fields.label: Agent Slug
fields.description: Stable registry key (e.g. audit-auditor). Used as the doc name (naming_rule By fieldname) so a re-sync is idempotent.

fields.label: Category
fields.description: Domain slug (audit, compliance, close, ap, ar, bank-recon, analytical-review).

fields.label: Delivery
fields.description: How the agent's SKILL body reaches the container. 'legacy': the body is stored in skill_bundle on this row and pushed from the bench (old path). 'delegate': the body NEVER enters the customer DB — the bench emits only an enablement signal and admin looks the body up from the bundle store (A2). Set by the catalog sync from registry.delivery; empty/absent registry = legacy.

fields.label: Tools Required (JSON)
fields.description: JSON array of jarvis__* tool ids the agent needs.

fields.label: DocTypes Required (JSON)
fields.description: JSON array of DocTypes the run-as user MUST hold read on for this agent's aggregates to be numerically correct (A12). Checked at install/validate against the mapped run_as_user (fail-closed on a missing read). Declared here so it is checkable without leaking rule shape.

fields.label: Rule Tokens (JSON)
fields.description: A2/A16 id-only rule token set for a delegate auditor: the OPAQUE tokens the container evaluator emits and record_agent_run validates a finding's rule against. Tokens only — NO rule bodies, statements, thresholds or predicates ever reach the bench. Empty for operators / legacy agents. Synced from registry.rule_tokens (itself mirrored from the bundle store's rules.ids.json).

fields.label: Min Apps (JSON)
fields.description: JSON array of required Frappe apps, e.g. ["erpnext"].

fields.label: Rule Pack
fields.description: Bundled rule-pack id evaluated by run_scrutiny (e.g. scrutiny-pack).

fields.label: Skill Bundle (JSON)
fields.description: JSON list of {path, body} SKILL.md payloads pushed to the container on Apply. permlevel 1 (SM-only; PART 3 TASK 33): the proprietary agent rule-pack is vendor IP, so a normal Jarvis User cannot read it via generic REST / get_agent. The catalog seed writes it via ignore_permissions and the engine reads it via get_doc (both bypass permlevel).

fields.label: Default Schedule (JSON)
fields.description: JSON {schedule_enabled, schedule_frequency} applied when the agent is installed.

fields.label: Validated For FY
fields.description: The fiscal year the (statutory) rule set was last legal-reviewed for. Used to gate stale statutory packs.

fields.label: Allowed Roles
fields.description: Roles allowed to install/run this agent. Empty = unrestricted (any user). System Manager is always allowed. Bench-admin state — NEVER written by the catalog sync.
